sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 17.41 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.17)

statement.latex: Let$S$be a solvable subgroup of a finite group$G$that has no nontrivial solvable normal subgroups. [r]{(a)} (L.\,Babai, A.\,J.\,Goodman, L.\,Pyber). Do there always exist seven conjugates of$S$whose intersection is trivial? [r]{(b)} Do there always exist five conjugates of$S$whose intersection is trivial? Editors' comments: Reduction of part (b) to almost simple group$G$is obtained in (E.\,P.\,Vdo...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory IV_PROBLEM_UNDEFINED; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
